page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the c4 server family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz with hyperthreading enabled. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core and text processing tests. Stress-ng single-core performance and text generation for small language models rank in the top 25%. Multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix math acceleration.

The primary tradeoff of the c4-highcpu-8 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio and the complete absence of local storage, which requires external block storage attachment. This profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not demand large memory footprints or local disk caching. It is suited for single-threaded processing tasks, text processing, lightweight web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized server configured with 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. Featuring 4 physical cores with hyperthreading, the instance provides a dedicated CPU allocation but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show top-tier results in single-threaded tasks, including gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core metrics, alongside strong text generation speeds for small language models. Conversely, multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database transactions perform in the average tier. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and no bundled local storage, this server represents a qualitative tradeoff favoring raw compute over memory capacity. It is best suited for compute-bound workloads such as text processing, lightweight web serving, and single-threaded data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance but lacking local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized server featuring 8 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, optimized for single-threaded execution and text processing workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and text processing benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for hardware acceleration
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Suited for single-threaded processing, text manipulation, and lightweight web serving
